text: The air is cool and bright, sterilized by glass walls and the hush of a modern building. Yet as I draw closer to the R+D exhibit, the words on the screen have a familiar sting. I feel strangely seen—vulnerable, as if my own uncertainties have been hung on display. The clinical space and the confessional text combine to stir in me a sense of both curiosity and awkward self-awareness. I am suspended between intrigue and the loneliness of misunderstood intentions.
